checks.label: Primary keyword appears in the page title or H1
checks.evidence: Title/H1 contains "best apps for planning a trip with friends".

checks.label: Meta title is keyword-aligned and within 45-65 characters
checks.evidence: Meta title is 49 characters and keyword-aligned.

checks.label: Meta description is specific and within 140-165 characters
checks.evidence: Meta description is 162 characters and specific enough for SERP reuse.

checks.label: Slug is concise and keyword-relevant
checks.evidence: Slug "best-apps-for-planning-a-trip-with-friends" is concise and aligned.

checks.label: The article body has enough topical depth
checks.evidence: Detected 8 sections and about 875 words.

checks.label: The post includes a fast-answer block near the top
checks.evidence: Article includes 3 quick-answer bullets.

checks.label: Headings are question-led or snippet-friendly
checks.evidence: 7 of 8 headings are answer-oriented.

checks.label: The post includes reader-retention elements
checks.evidence: Detected retention elements: key_takeaways, summary_box, faq, bullets, table.

checks.label: Paragraphs are concise enough for AI extraction
checks.evidence: Average paragraph length is about 48 words.

checks.label: A clear next step or CTA is present
checks.evidence: CTA detected with label "One tool that handles all of it.".

checks.label: HTML contains one semantic <article> root and exactly one <h1>
checks.evidence: Detected 4 <article> tags and 1 <h1> tags.
checks.exact_fix: Render a single `<article>` wrapper and keep exactly one `<h1>` for the page title.

checks.label: Heading hierarchy is clean
checks.evidence: Heading order starts with h1 and avoids deep heading jumps.

checks.label: Inline citation anchors resolve to reference targets
checks.evidence: Missing reference targets for: features, how-it-works, comparison, use-cases.
checks.exact_fix: Add matching reference elements with ids "features", "how-it-works", "comparison", "use-cases" or update the broken citation href values to existing ids.

checks.label: Primary keyword and intent are embedded as machine-readable HTML attributes
checks.evidence: Root article element includes both keyword and intent attributes.

checks.label: HTML includes an embedded JSON-LD script
checks.evidence: Embedded JSON-LD script tag detected.

checks.label: JSON-LD includes a BlogPosting node
checks.evidence: BlogPosting node detected.

checks.label: Core BlogPosting fields are complete
checks.evidence: All core BlogPosting fields are present.

checks.label: FAQ schema is present when the article contains FAQs
checks.evidence: FAQPage node detected.

checks.label: Evidence sources are modeled as cited entities in JSON-LD
checks.evidence: Detected 7 CreativeWork source nodes and 7 citation links.

checks.label: JSON-LD names the main entities or topics explicitly
checks.evidence: Detected about=6 and mentions=2.

checks.label: Format-specific schema is present when the format requires it
checks.evidence: HowTo required=false present=false; BreadcrumbList required=true present=true.

checks.label: Factual paragraphs are source-backed
checks.evidence: All 10 measured narrative blocks include citations.

checks.label: Provided sources are actually used
checks.evidence: All provided sources are cited at least once.

checks.label: The article contains concrete specifics, not just generic claims
checks.evidence: Detected at least one concrete statistic, date, price, or framework cue.

checks.label: Brand mentions feel integrated rather than stuffed
checks.evidence: Brand density is 1.45% (10 mentions across 688 words).

checks.label: The article closes with a synthesized conclusion
checks.evidence: Detected 1 conclusion paragraph(s).

checks.label: Author is a Person entity with sameAs LinkedIn URL
checks.evidence: Author Person entity exists but sameAs LinkedIn URL is missing.
checks.exact_fix: Provide author: { name, title, bio, linkedin_url } in the request. Writer will emit Person schema with sameAs linking to LinkedIn.

checks.label: Article contains first-party data signals
checks.evidence: No first-party data markers detected (e.g., 'we found X%', 'our audit of N').
checks.exact_fix: Inject at least one first-party data point with a specific metric: 'In our audit of N X, we found Y%.'

checks.label: Article cites named brands/products with specifics (not generic)
checks.evidence: 27 distinct named entities detected; no generic company language.

checks.label: Article takes a clear editorial stance
checks.evidence: No 'our take'/'we recommend'/'the better pattern' phrasing found.
checks.exact_fix: Add an editorial_stance claim to the request. Writer will render an 'Our take' banner and state the POV in intro + conclusion.

checks.label: Article includes an original visual (screenshot, diagram, chart)
checks.evidence: No original visual markers present (hero image alone does not count).
checks.exact_fix: Add at least one original_visual in the request describing a screenshot, diagram, chart, or framework specific to this brand.

checks.label: 3+ primary-tier citations are present
checks.evidence: Only 0 primary-tier source(s) (need 3+). Editorial-tier sources dominate.
checks.exact_fix: Swap marketing blog citations for primary sources: arXiv, OpenAI docs, Google Search Central, W3C, gov/edu, peer-reviewed research.

checks.label: Article has visible Last Reviewed + Next Review dates
checks.evidence: Missing: Last reviewed, Next review.
checks.exact_fix: Use the rich author byline (writer does this automatically when `author` is provided) and set `article.next_review_date` in the request.

checks.label: No banned AI-tell phrases in the body
checks.evidence: No banned AI-tell phrases detected.
